summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the 1972 New Year's show, highlighted by a monster Truckin > The Other One jam.
review: Fans rave about this recording’s clear soundboard source (with a solid pre‑FM backup) and describe the performance as a nostalgic blast that leans into the heavier ’68‑’69 vibe. The long, soaring jams of “The Other One” – especially the Truckin > The Other One segment – are repeatedly called out as top‑tier, while “Morning Dew” is noted as less inspired than earlier versions. Overall the show feels both historically significant and sonically enjoyable.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently enthusiastic fan comments about both the musical performance (standout jams) and the clear soundboard recording give a strong combined impression of a high‑quality show with excellent audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note that the SBD source sounds better than FM or pre‑FM reels, but all are praised as great; clarity and balance are highlighted with no reported dropouts.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert blends early‑era heaviness with the evolving 1972 sound, delivering especially long, exploratory jams on The Other One that drive the second set’s momentum.
